[2018-03-02 00:42] steel0229e (Steel): legend was like 1% of the hs palyerbase
[2018-03-02 00:42] steel0229e (Steel): and 0,5% of the starcrfat palyerbase
[2018-03-02 00:42] steel0229e (Steel): so I assume you would not reach legend unless you tried hardcore and payed
[2018-03-02 00:42] steel0229e (Steel): many people assume it is easy, yet the numbers say otherwise
[2018-03-02 00:42] liadahlia (Lia): it's not necessarily that hard
[2018-03-02 00:43] awaclus: yeah but there are so many n00bs playing HS
[2018-03-02 00:43] steel0229e (Steel): it is a grind but not many people have the dedication
[2018-03-02 00:43] gryph3155 (Gryph202): I'm so lukewarm on hearthstone.  Blah.
[2018-03-02 00:43] liadahlia (Lia): you can easily autopilot a deck with ~52% winrate
[2018-03-02 00:43] liadahlia (Lia): and you just grind the statistics
[2018-03-02 00:43] steel0229e (Steel): but still it is kinda an elite group because it takes so much effort
[2018-03-02 00:44] steel0229e (Steel): I personally dont think it means much
[2018-03-02 00:44] steel0229e (Steel): I've been multiple legend and I agree it is a grind mostly
[2018-03-02 00:44] steel0229e (Steel): but game knowledge correlates with being legend in my experience
[2018-03-02 00:45] steel0229e (Steel): you don't see as many weird misplays the higher your rank is
[2018-03-02 00:45] mqp: why is it easy to find a deck with 52% winrate?
[2018-03-02 00:46] mqp: isn't the meta trying to make that hard?
[2018-03-02 00:46] mqp: (i never tried constructed hearthstone)
[2018-03-02 00:46] gryph3155 (Gryph202): A 52% win rate still takes effort IMO.
[2018-03-02 00:46] steel0229e (Steel): yeah by default 50% is what you can achieve with most netdecks with average skill
[2018-03-02 00:46] steel0229e (Steel): there are also a large number of people so your winrate is naturally being reduced
[2018-03-02 00:47] awaclus: how does the number of people reduce your winrate?
[2018-03-02 00:47] steel0229e (Steel): more copy paste so your netdeck is less useful
[2018-03-02 00:48] steel0229e (Steel): there develops a rock-paper-scissors meta game so your EV is 50%
[2018-03-02 00:48] liadahlia (Lia): you gotta factor game speed into the equation
[2018-03-02 00:48] liadahlia (Lia): so playing a fast deck gets you to legend faster
[2018-03-02 00:48] liadahlia (Lia): aka if you play face decks or zoo
[2018-03-02 00:49] steel0229e (Steel): also more  people= more even distribution of skill
[2018-03-02 00:49] liadahlia (Lia): so traditionally zoo has always had a high winrate vs the rest of the decks + it's fast
[2018-03-02 00:49] steel0229e (Steel): so you are expected to luck streak less based on skill difference
[2018-03-02 00:49] liadahlia (Lia): so that's what people usually go for when they want to ladder really fast
[2018-03-02 00:49] liadahlia (Lia): and because of win streak
[2018-03-02 00:49] liadahlia (Lia): if you have a pure 50% winrate,
[2018-03-02 00:50] liadahlia (Lia): eventually you will get to legend
[2018-03-02 00:50] awaclus: you're expected to luck streak less than in Prismata?
[2018-03-02 00:50] liadahlia (Lia): because if you win three times in a row, you get +1 more than if you lose 3 games in a row
[2018-03-02 00:50] steel0229e (Steel): Awaclus yes because Prismata has less players
[2018-03-02 00:50] steel0229e (Steel): you might get freelo if you can beat someone 100% and repeatedly get matched because ladder is empty
[2018-03-02 00:51] awaclus: you don't get very much elo if you beat them 100% though
[2018-03-02 00:51] steel0229e (Steel): but assume you start from tier 1
[2018-03-02 00:51] steel0229e (Steel): you have steady progression
[2018-03-02 00:52] steel0229e (Steel): while in HS you can't streak because of this
[2018-03-02 00:52] gryph3155 (Gryph202): @Steel I fully expect those figures to skew heavily once we're released and anyone can play.  😛
[2018-03-02 00:52] steel0229e (Steel): it's already skewed, it was like this more like 2-3 years ago
[2018-03-02 00:53] steel0229e (Steel): also if there is a rating difference of 500 eloyot between you and your opponent queueing, the two of you wont even get matched. ever
[2018-03-02 00:53] steel0229e (Steel): so you won't face any top players on ladder
[2018-03-02 00:53] steel0229e (Steel): I just tried to explain why I said that HS having more palyers brings your winrate closer to 50%
[2018-03-02 00:54] steel0229e (Steel): that is why I brought this up
[2018-03-02 00:54] gryph3155 (Gryph202): Just saying, I think it will be closer to 50% after release than before.
[2018-03-02 00:54] wilson0862 (Wilson): You aren't disagreeing with each other.

[2018-03-02 00:55] gryph3155 (Gryph202): my largest concern is infrastructure, quite frankly.  I just hope that the server will be able to support the influx of new players.
[2018-03-02 00:55] steel0229e (Steel): and the global chat can keep up lol
[2018-03-02 00:56] gryph3155 (Gryph202): Outside of that, I'm not terribly ambitious.  Just ask @Steel what a lousy player I am.  LOL
[2018-03-02 00:56] steel0229e (Steel): you are not any worse than an average beginner
[2018-03-02 00:57] steel0229e (Steel): if you keep playing you will improve, even get tier x eventually
[2018-03-02 00:58] steel0229e (Steel): at first noone has a clue and everyone feels overwhelmed
[2018-03-02 00:58] liadahlia (Lia): tbh i'm sure elyot has thought of everything
[2018-03-02 00:59] gryph3155 (Gryph202): Yeah, that doesn't mean that everything will go smoothly.
[2018-03-02 00:59] steel0229e (Steel): i'd say the new player onboarding system is decent
[2018-03-02 01:00] steel0229e (Steel): and I think it adds to the game's value that it takes time to get into it
[2018-03-02 01:00] liadahlia (Lia): i'm very hyped for the campaign
[2018-03-02 01:00] liadahlia (Lia): i want to know what crazy shit they came up with
[2018-03-02 01:01] steel0229e (Steel): Elyot once said that even a player who only has 10 games is almost always expected to beat someone with 0 games
[2018-03-02 01:01] dunkoro: I mean, for most card games people *think* they've gotten into them by 10h
[2018-03-02 01:01] liadahlia (Lia): oh yeah i remember that
[2018-03-02 01:01] dunkoro: when it actually takes 100+h
[2018-03-02 01:01] liadahlia (Lia): i read that reddit thread like yesterday while googling some stuff
[2018-03-02 01:01] dunkoro: I'd say with prismata it's just more apparent that you're still a newbie than it is with most games because of how punishing severe mistakes are
[2018-03-02 01:02] dunkoro: but it doesn't actually take more time to become well-versed in the game itself
[2018-03-02 01:02] steel0229e (Steel): yes it rewards skill a lot, I can compare it with Starcraft
[2018-03-02 01:02] liadahlia (Lia): <https://www.reddit.com/r/Prismata/comments/2m0ihp/why_i_dont_play_prismata/>
[2018-03-02 01:02] steel0229e (Steel): and it is enjoyable at all skill levels, you dont need to invest a lot of time to have fun
[2018-03-02 01:02] steel0229e (Steel): if you find an equal match
[2018-03-02 01:02] liadahlia (Lia): Steel, elyot said that in this thread ^
[2018-03-02 01:03] mqp: experienced board game players should be the equivalent of 1500 by about twenty games in
[2018-03-02 01:03] mqp: that's a pretty shallow learning curve
[2018-03-02 01:04] mqp: (since games are pretty short)
[2018-03-02 01:04] liadahlia (Lia): i think it has more to do with very complex mechanics that makes more sense once you have 20 games in
[2018-03-02 01:04] liadahlia (Lia): so maybe not steep, but mechanics that are different from other games
[2018-03-02 01:05] dunkoro: eh, I happen to be friends with one relatively experienced board game player
[2018-03-02 01:05] liadahlia (Lia): for example, absorb made no sense to me when i started out
[2018-03-02 01:05] dunkoro: and he has trouble beating master bot
[2018-03-02 01:05] dunkoro: so I am not sure 1500 is actually that easy
[2018-03-02 01:05] dunkoro: master bot was measured to be what, around 1400?
[2018-03-02 01:05] mqp: ah ok i am thinking of roughly master bot level whatever that is
[2018-03-02 01:05] steel0229e (Steel): which is between 1400 and 1500 i'd say
[2018-03-02 01:06] mqp: where you know to drone up, build an absorber, defend on it, and spend your resources every turn
[2018-03-02 01:06] liadahlia (Lia): i think an obvious milestone for any new player is to beat master bot consistently
[2018-03-02 01:06] liadahlia (Lia): but then the problem is right now, there's such a huge jump from master bot to ladder
[2018-03-02 01:06] dunkoro: because ladder starts at 1600+
[2018-03-02 01:06] liadahlia (Lia): larger playerbase should fix that
[2018-03-02 01:07] dunkoro: prismata might stay niche enough that the playerbase good matchmaking requires just won't get here
[2018-03-02 01:07] mqp: seems unlikely, already often there is one match with someone tier 9 in it at any given moment during the day
[2018-03-02 01:07] liadahlia (Lia): i'm afraid prismata will become battlerite
[2018-03-02 01:07] liadahlia (Lia): game's very hard and niche
[2018-03-02 01:07] mqp: all you need is 5x and then you will usually be able to find an opponent as a beginner
[2018-03-02 01:08] mqp: if elyot can't 5x the userbase by actually releasing the game then i will be surprised
[2018-03-02 01:08] liadahlia (Lia): it will get a ~4500 players or something and slowly dwindle down to 1000
[2018-03-02 01:08] gryph3155 (Gryph202): That's why I want to get as many new players as I can from OBG.
[2018-03-02 01:08] dunkoro: nah, I think prismata can keep a stable 5k playerbase
